---
title: "Sandbox"
description: "Choose when code should run in eve's isolated workspace and how to configure it."
---

Use the sandbox for work the model controls: shell commands, generated files, cloned repositories,
and temporary analysis. Every eve agent has one isolated filesystem rooted at `/workspace`, and the
built-in `bash`, `read_file`, `write_file`, `glob`, and `grep` tools use it automatically.

Authored tools do **not** run there. We keep their `execute` functions in the eve server process so
application integrations can use server dependencies and credentials. Call `ctx.getSandbox()` only
when an authored tool intentionally needs isolated execution.

```ts title="agent/tools/count_rows.ts"
import { defineTool } from "eve/tools";
import { z } from "zod";

export default defineTool({
  description: "Count the lines in a file in the sandbox workspace.",
  inputSchema: z.object({ path: z.string() }),
  async execute({ path }, ctx) {
    const sandbox = await ctx.getSandbox();
    const result = await sandbox.run({ command: `wc -l ${JSON.stringify(path)}` });

    if (result.exitCode !== 0) {
      throw new Error(result.stderr || `wc exited with ${result.exitCode}`);
    }

    return { stdout: result.stdout.trim() };
  },
});
```

`ctx.getSandbox()` is asynchronous and is available only while an authored runtime callback is running. Relative paths resolve from `/workspace`.

## Default tools

| Tool         | Operation                              |
| ------------ | -------------------------------------- |
| `bash`       | Run a command and wait for it to exit. |
| `read_file`  | Read a text file under `/workspace`.   |
| `write_file` | Write a text file under `/workspace`.  |
| `glob`       | Find workspace paths by glob pattern.  |
| `grep`       | Search workspace file contents.        |

The sandbox handle also exposes `run`, `spawn`, text and binary file methods, `removePath`, `resolvePath`, and `setNetworkPolicy`. Import their option and result types from `eve/sandbox`.

Use `spawn` for a process that must stay alive while the agent continues:

```ts
const sandbox = await ctx.getSandbox();
const server = await sandbox.spawn({ command: "node server.mjs" });

// Inspect server.stdout or do other work.

await server.kill();
```

## Seed the workspace

Files under `agent/sandbox/workspace/` are copied into `/workspace` when a session sandbox is created:

```text
agent/sandbox/
  sandbox.ts
  workspace/
    schema.sql
    scripts/
      summarize.mjs
```

The example produces `/workspace/schema.sql` and `/workspace/scripts/summarize.mjs`. Do not seed `workspace/skills`; eve reserves `/workspace/skills` for authored [skills](../skills).

The `agent/sandbox/workspace/` directory is sufficient to seed files; `sandbox.ts` inside that directory is optional. If you also need a definition, put it at `agent/sandbox/sandbox.ts`. The top-level shorthand `agent/sandbox.ts` is available only when there is no `agent/sandbox/` directory.

```ts title="agent/sandbox/sandbox.ts"
import { defineSandbox } from "eve/sandbox";

export default defineSandbox({});
```

You do not need this file for the default sandbox. Add it to select a fixed [backend](./backends) or configure [bootstrap, persistence, and network policy](./lifecycle-and-network).

## Isolation boundary

> **Security consequence:** Treat model-authored commands and files as untrusted. Keep application
> secrets in the server process, return only the data the model needs, and use an egress policy for
> production workloads. A sandbox limits what model-controlled code can reach; it does not decide
> which users may invoke your agent. Configure inbound identity separately in
> [Authentication](../../operate/authentication).
